title: Velvety Adventurous Irish Apricot Stir-fry
description: This dish combines the savory depth of broiling with the sweetness and intrigue of dried apricots, making it a delightful and adventurous Irish meal that's also fodmap-friendly.

ingredients.name: 1/4 cup low-sugar soy sauce or tamari
ingredients.notes: For a sugar-free option, use agave nectar or stevia.

instructions: Preheat your broiler according to the manufacturer’s instructions.
In a large skillet or wok, heat a tablespoon of oil over medium-high heat.
Add the minced garlic and cook for about 30 seconds until fragrant but not browned.
Fold in the chopped dried apricots. Stir-fry for about 5 minutes or until they start to release their moisture.
Add the lime juice, low-sugar soy sauce (or tamari), salt, and pepper. Stir well to combine.
For a velvety texture, press the mixture through a fine-mesh sieve into the skillet and stir gently. This helps remove any seeds or tough fibers from the apricots.
If desired, drizzle in the optional low-sodium soy sauce for extra depth of flavor.
Cook over medium-high heat until the liquid has slightly reduced and the flavors are well combined, about 3-5 minutes more.
Serve hot as a side dish or main course depending on your meal plan.
